name: Mexican Pot Roast
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons tomato paste, mixed with broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a 4 to 5 quart ovenproof pot over medium heat.
2. Pat the meat dry with paper towels. Add it to the hot oil along with green onion and garlic.
3. Brown the meat on all sides, stirring occasionally.
4. Add beef broth mixed with tomato paste and all seasonings. Cover tightly.
5. Bake for 2 1/2 to 3 hours in a preheated oven at 325 degrees Fahrenheit until the meat is fork tender.
6. Lift the meat onto a cutting board. Cover loosely with foil and let stand for 10 to 15 minutes.
7. Skim the fat from the sauce. If desired, thicken the sauce by stirring in 1 square of unsweetened chocolate.
8. Slice the meat across the grain and serve with the sauce.
